text:
There once was a wise old owl named OWLY. He lived high in the ancient oak forest. One moonlit night, OWLY decided to teachittle sleepy sleepy kittens how to hunt.

He led them near a heap of old feathers. "Look," he whispered. "That's owl-down."

"A feather!" squeaked little OWLY.

The Owls hunted late. Owls listen hard. They found a chipmunk!

"Quick!" Owchy cried.

They snatched him.

Back in feathers.

Then they fed.

Good friends.

Later, owl feathers blew away.

They floated.

Near Owltown.

That's where.

They're still.

I think.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
